que: In Use Cases in Domain Layer, what is the primary goal?
options: replace testing entirely
encapsulate single business actions
skip state/data modeling
avoid understanding requirements

que: Which API/concept is most central to Use Cases in Domain Layer?
options: manual polling loop for all tasks
random utility class name
legacy XML-only patterns unrelated to this topic
UseCase class

que: What is a recommended best practice for Use Cases in Domain Layer?
options: optimize before verifying correctness
mix unrelated responsibilities in one function
ignore edge/error states
keep use cases focused and framework-agnostic

que: Which is a common mistake when implementing Use Cases in Domain Layer?
options: keep boundaries clear
follow official docs for API usage
placing business rules in UI controllers
write focused tests for behavior

que: For production code, which approach fits Use Cases in Domain Layer best?
options: Use Use Cases in Domain Layer while bypassing architecture boundaries
Use Use Cases in Domain Layer only as theory with no implementation
Use Use Cases in Domain Layer and suppress all warnings
Use Use Cases in Domain Layer with clear state flow and testable logic

que: A teammate asks where to start with Use Cases in Domain Layer. What is best advice?
options: skip running the app after changes
copy an unknown snippet without context
avoid feedback from tests/review
Read official docs (https://developer.android.com/topic/architecture/domain-layer) and implement a small sample

que: Which statement about Use Cases in Domain Layer is most accurate?
options: Use Cases in Domain Layer removes the need for any testing
Use Cases in Domain Layer is most effective when paired with clear ownership of state/data
Use Cases in Domain Layer is mainly about adding boilerplate
Use Cases in Domain Layer should be applied identically in every layer

que: In a code review for Use Cases in Domain Layer, what would you flag?
options: Code that hides failures instead of handling them explicitly
traceable data flow between layers
small pure functions with clear names
explicit modeling of UI/data states

que: Which scenario is the strongest example of using Use Cases in Domain Layer correctly?
options: A small, working feature that demonstrates UseCase class in context
a large refactor without tests
an implementation with no error states
a slide deck with no runnable code

que: To learn Use Cases in Domain Layer deeply, what should you do next?
options: skip verification to save time
Build, test, profile (if needed), and iterate using Use Cases in Domain Layer patterns
avoid official references
memorize definitions only
